---
title: "Unconfoundedness and Propensity Scores"
---

## Beyond a single RCT

Consider now two RCTs as follows:

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| label: two rct tables
g1 <- tibble::tibble(
  "Palo Alto" = c('treated', 'control')
  , "non-smoker" = c(152, 2362)
  , "smoker" = c(5,122)
) |> gt::gt() |> 
  gt::cols_width(everything() ~ gt::px(98)) |> gtExtras::gt_theme_espn()

g2 <- tibble::tibble(
  "Geneva" = c('treated', 'control')
  , "non-smoker" = c(581, 2278)
  , "smoker" = c(350, 1979)
) |> gt::gt() |> 
  gt::cols_width(everything() ~ gt::px(98)) |> gtExtras::gt_theme_espn()
gt::gt_group(
  g1, g2
) 
```

To estimate the ATE, we need to estimate treatment effects within each city (i.e. $\hat{\tau}_{PA}, \hat{\tau}_{GVA}$) using the difference-in-means estimator, and then weight the results according to the number of participants in each city

$$
\hat{\tau}=\frac{2641}{2641+5188}\hat{\tau}_{PA}+\frac{5188}{2641+5188}\hat{\tau}_{GVA}
$$

Palo Alto treatment effect calculation: $\hat{\tau}_{PA} = \frac{5}{152 + 5} - \frac{122}{2362 + 122} \approx -1.7\%$

Geneva treatment effect calculation: $\hat{\tau}_{GVA} = \frac{350}{350 + 581} - \frac{1979}{2278 + 1979} \approx -8.9\%$

Combined estimate: $\hat{\tau} = \frac{2641}{2641 + 5188} \hat{\tau}_{PA} + \frac{5188}{2641 + 5188} \hat{\tau}_{GVA} \approx -6.5\%$

## Stratified estimation

Formalizing this approach, suppose we have covariates in a finite discrete space $X_i\in\mathcal{X}$ and that treatment is random conditionally on $X_i$ (i.e. we have an RCT for each group defined by $X_i$).

The assumption of conditional independence (unconfoundedness) is:

$$
\{Y_i(0), Y_i(1)\} \perp D_i | X_i = x, \quad \text{for all } x \in \mathcal{X}
$$

or in more general form: $\{Y_i(0), Y_i(1)\} \perp D_i | X_i$

then the *Conditional Average Treatment Effect* (CATE) is

$$
\tau(x) = \mathbb{E}[Y_i(1) - Y_i(0) | X_i = x]
$$

and the stratified estimator is:

$$
\hat{\tau}_{STRAT} = \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} \frac{n_x}{n} \hat{\tau}(x)
$$

where:

$$
\begin{align*}
\hat{\tau}(x) & =\frac{1}{n_{x,1}}\sum_{\{X_{i}=x,D_{i}=1\}}Y_{i}-\frac{1}{n_{x,0}}\sum_{\{X_{i}=x,D_{i}=0\}}Y_{i}\\
 & =\frac{1}{n_{x}}\sum_{\{X_{i}=x\}}\frac{D_{i}Y_{i}}{\hat{e}(x)}-\frac{\left(1-D_{i}\right)Y_{i}}{1-\hat{e}(x)}
\end{align*}
$$

Notation:

-   $n_x = |\{i : X_i = x\}|$ (number in each strata)
-   $n_{x,d} = |\{i : X_i = x, D_i = d\}|$ (number treated each strata)
-   $\hat{e}(x) = \frac{n_{x,1}}{n_x}$ (estimated probability of treatment)

## Theorem 2.1 (Stratified Estimation)

Central limit theorem for stratified estimator: $\sqrt{n}(\hat{\tau}_{STRAT} - \tau) \Rightarrow N(0, V_{STRAT})$

Asymptotic variance: $V_{STRAT} = \text{Var}[\tau(X_i)] + \mathbb{E}\left[\frac{\sigma^2_{(1)}(X_i)}{e(X_i)} + \frac{\sigma^2_{(0)}(X_i)}{1-e(X_i)}\right]$

where:

-   $e(x) = \mathbb{P}[D_i = 1 | X_i = x]$ (propensity score)

-   $\sigma^2_{(d)}(x) = \text{Var}[Y_i^{(d)} | X_i = x]$

Decomposition of stratified estimator:

$$
\hat{\tau}_{STRAT} = \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} \lambda(x)\tau(x) + \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} (\hat{\lambda}(x) - \lambda(x)) \tau(x) + \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} \lambda(x) (\hat{\tau}(x) - \tau(x)) + \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} (\hat{\lambda}(x) - \lambda(x)) (\hat{\tau}(x) - \tau(x))
$$

where:

-   $\lambda(x) = \mathbb{P}[X_i = x]$ (true probability)
-   $\hat{\lambda}(x) = n_x/n$ (empirical probability)

## Propensity Score

Definition of propensity score: $e(x) = \mathbb{P}[D_i = 1 | X_i = x]$

Balancing property: $\{Y_i(0), Y_i(1)\} \perp D_i | e(X_i)$

Proof of balancing property: $\begin{align}
\mathbb{P}[D_i = w | \{Y_i(w)\}, e(X_i)] &= \int_{\mathcal{X}} \mathbb{P}[D_i = w | \{Y_i(w)\}, X_i = x] \mathbb{P}[X_i = x | \{Y_i(w)\}, e(X_i)] dx \\
&= \int_{\mathcal{X}} \mathbb{P}[D_i = w | X_i = x] \mathbb{P}[X_i = x | \{Y_i(w)\}, e(X_i)] dx \quad \text{(unconf.)} \\
&= \begin{cases}
e(X_i) & \text{if } w = 1 \\
1 - e(X_i) & \text{else}
\end{cases}
\end{align}$

## Propensity Stratification

Sorting step: $\hat{e}(X_{i_1}) \leq \hat{e}(X_{i_2}) \leq \ldots \leq \hat{e}(X_{i_n})$

Stratum-specific estimator: $\hat{\tau}_j = \frac{\sum_{j=\lfloor(j-1)n/J\rfloor+1}^{\lfloor jn/J\rfloor} D_i Y_i}{\sum_{j=\lfloor(j-1)n/J\rfloor+1}^{\lfloor jn/J\rfloor} D_i} - \frac{\sum_{j=\lfloor(j-1)n/J\rfloor+1}^{\lfloor jn/J\rfloor} (1-D_i) Y_i}{\sum_{j=\lfloor(j-1)n/J\rfloor+1}^{\lfloor jn/J\rfloor} (1-D_i)}$

Propensity stratification estimator: $\hat{\tau}_{PSTRAT} = \frac{1}{J} \sum_{j=1}^J \hat{\tau}_j$

## Inverse-Propensity Weighting (IPW)

IPW estimator with estimated propensity scores: $\hat{\tau}_{IPW} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \left(\frac{D_i Y_i}{\hat{e}(X_i)} - \frac{(1-D_i) Y_i}{1-\hat{e}(X_i)}\right)$

Oracle IPW estimator (true propensity scores): $\hat{\tau}^*_{IPW} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \left(\frac{D_i Y_i}{e(X_i)} - \frac{(1-D_i) Y_i}{1-e(X_i)}\right)$

## Theorem 2.2 (Oracle IPW)

Central limit theorem for oracle IPW: $\sqrt{n}(\hat{\tau}^*_{IPW} - \tau) \Rightarrow N(0, V_{IPW^*})$

Asymptotic variance: $V_{IPW^*} = \text{Var}[\tau(X_i)] + \mathbb{E}\left[\frac{(\mu_{(0)}(X_i) + (1-e(X_i))\tau(X_i))^2}{e(X_i)(1-e(X_i))}\right] + \mathbb{E}\left[\frac{\sigma^2_{(1)}(X_i)}{e(X_i)} + \frac{\sigma^2_{(0)}(X_i)}{1-e(X_i)}\right]$

Alternative decomposition in proof: $\frac{D_i Y_i}{e(X_i)} - \frac{(1-D_i) Y_i}{1-e(X_i)} = \tau(X_i) + \left(\frac{D_i}{e(X_i)} - \frac{1-D_i}{1-e(X_i)}\right) c(X_i) + \frac{D_i \varepsilon_i^{(1)}}{e(X_i)} - \frac{(1-D_i) \varepsilon_i^{(0)}}{1-e(X_i)}$

where: - $c(x) = \mu_{(0)}(x) + (1-e(x))\tau(x)$ - $\varepsilon_i^{(w)} = Y_i^{(w)} - \mu_{(w)}(X_i)$

## Variance Comparison

Relationship between IPW and stratified variances: $V_{IPW^*} = V_{STRAT} + \mathbb{E}\left[\frac{(\mu_{(0)}(X_i) + (1-e(X_i))\tau(X_i))^2}{e(X_i)(1-e(X_i))}\right]$

Therefore: $V_{IPW^*} \geq V_{STRAT}$ (IPW is generally less efficient than stratification)

## Overlap Assumptions

Weak overlap: $\mathbb{E}[1/(e(X_i)(1-e(X_i)))] < \infty$

Strong overlap: $\eta \leq e(x) \leq 1-\eta \quad \text{for all } x \in \mathcal{X}$

for some $\eta > 0$.

## Connection Between Stratified and IPW

When $\mathcal{X}$ is discrete, the stratified estimator can be written as IPW: $\hat{\tau}_{STRAT} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \left(\frac{D_i Y_i}{\hat{e}(X_i)} - \frac{(1-D_i) Y_i}{1-\hat{e}(X_i)}\right)$

where: $\hat{e}(x) = \frac{n_{x1}}{n_x}$

## Auxiliary Functions and Notation

Conditional mean functions: $\mu^{(w)}(x) = \mathbb{E}[Y_i^{(w)} | X_i = x]$

Conditional variance functions: $\sigma^{(w)2}(x) = \text{Var}[Y_i^{(w)} | X_i = x]$

Sample sizes by stratum and treatment: - $n_x = |\{i : X_i = x\}|$ (total in stratum $x$) - $n_{x1} = |\{i : X_i = x, D_i = 1\}|$ (treated in stratum $x$)\
- $n_{x0} = |\{i : X_i = x, D_i = 0\}|$ (control in stratum $x$)

## Estimation Error Terms

Decomposition of stratified estimator: $\hat{\tau}_{STRAT} = \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} \lambda(x)\tau(x) + \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} (\hat{\lambda}(x) - \lambda(x)) \tau(x) + \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} \lambda(x) (\hat{\tau}(x) - \tau(x)) + \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} (\hat{\lambda}(x) - \lambda(x)) (\hat{\tau}(x) - \tau(x))$

where: - $\lambda(x) = \mathbb{P}[X_i = x]$ (true probability) - $\hat{\lambda}(x) = n_x/n$ (empirical probability)

## Examples from Text
